messages.author: Aqueum

messages.author: Aqueum
messages.text: Has anyone had any success putting repeaters up munros or Scottish hills?

messages.author: Cupra0
messages.text: You would need to make solar for them, and some sort of tracker, if they go missing at least you can track them.

messages.reply_html:
Aqueum

messages.author: Aqueum
messages.text: Yea definitely solar. But Heltec sell panels for $10. I hadn’t thought about putting the GPS modules in, but I guess that makes sense. I was going to ask if people have had problems with theft. I imagine putting them up a big concreted-in poll would help avoid theft but also reduce likelihood that you’d get permission.
messages.content_html: Yea definitely solar. But Heltec sell panels for $10. I hadn’t thought about putting the GPS modules in, but I guess that makes sense. I was going to ask if people have had problems with theft. I imagine putting them up a big concreted-in poll would help avoid theft but also reduce likelihood that you’d get permission.

messages.author: Aqueum
messages.text: Here’s most of Fife and the Central Belt within line of site of 4 hilltop repeaters: Corse Hill, Loudoun Hill, Goat Fell and Allermuir Hill.
messages.content_html: Here’s most of Fife and the Central Belt within line of site of 4 hilltop repeaters: Corse Hill, Loudoun Hill, Goat Fell and Allermuir Hill.
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: Aqueum
messages.text: Adding Earl’s Seat fills a lot of gaps.
messages.content_html: Adding Earl’s Seat fills a lot of gaps.

messages.author: Aqueum
messages.text: Adding King’s Seat (G) brings much of Perth and Dundee to the party. Linking NE with Goyle Hill (H) then a couple more to cover Aberdeen and I think nearly 70% of Scotland’s population is painted red.
messages.content_html: Adding King’s Seat (G) brings much of Perth and Dundee to the party. Linking NE with Goyle Hill (H) then a couple more to cover Aberdeen and I think nearly 70% of Scotland’s population is painted red.
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: Aqueum
messages.text: My connection dropped (usually happens more quickly in the app than with the web client). It was doing so well that I kept going: Adding (F) Ben Rinnes and (J) Ord Hill lets us paint Inverness. Aberdeen would probably be better served with a Torry or Tullos Hill repeater than (L), or one at the Crematorium that may bring redundancy to the link from (H) Goyle Hill. Either way it still seems to need two. I think this paints the most populous 21 towns/cities in Scotland. 22 would be Dumfries which already has 2 repeaters on the MeshCore map and could hopefully join the above via a repeater on Lowther Hill. Then we’ve painted the top 40!
messages.content_html: My connection dropped (usually happens more quickly in the app than with the web client). messages.author: Aqueum
messages.text: Sorry, having quite the conversation with my self here… It was late at night. If it all seems too good to be true it probably is Liam suggested it may only be showing line of sight from each point, not taking into account propagation. I’ve had a look at the source code and it can take more into account, so when I get time I’ll try to paint a more conservative picture.
messages.content_html: Sorry, having quite the conversation with my self here… It was late at night. If it all seems too good to be true it probably is Liam suggested it may only be showing line of sight from each point, not taking into account propagation. I’ve had a look at the source code and it can take more into account, so when I get time I’ll try to paint a more conservative picture.
messages.reactions_html: 1

Aberdeen would probably be better served with a Torry or Tullos Hill repeater than (L), or one at the Crematorium that may bring redundancy to the link from (H) Goyle Hill. messages.author: George-GM1DSV
messages.text: Looking at your coverage forecast, I am within range... well at least under the red cloud . The iGate is for licensed amateur radio LoRa APRS and Internet linked to the global stuff. I like Meshcore as it is 100% off-grid and self supporting. I just hope @andykirby doesn't get overloaded as he really can fire out the firmware fixes quickly, some commercial groups could learn a lesson or two there. Mmmm, coffee sounds good mate. (edited)
messages.content_html: Looking at your coverage forecast, I am within range... well at least under the red cloud . The iGate is for licensed amateur radio LoRa APRS and Internet linked to the global stuff. I like Meshcore as it is 100% off-grid and self supporting. I just hope @andykirby doesn't get overloaded as he really can fire out the firmware fixes quickly, some commercial groups could learn a lesson or two there. Mmmm, coffee sounds good mate. (edited)
messages.reply_html:
bosgag
It's all new to me, I currently use the system for tracking my dogs on the hill but haven't looked at igates at all. If you fancy a coffee sometime to chat over just say the word

messages.author: Captain Eggbeard
messages.text: I'm willing to chip in for components and help get these hilltop repeaters set up. Not sure if it's any good for mounting radio equipment to, but I've got an old clothes drying pole that was dug up in the garden. I don't think we can go around hills cementing old poles into the ground though. Is there a proper way to go about it?

messages.author: bosgag
messages.text: I cold called one of the land owners and he's thinking about it and will be going down to see him in a week or 2 to show him the set-up and maybe agree a location. I also accosted theocak farmer here and got his agreement to use the hill behind my house. It would be good to get other hills to extend the network but also have more localised for redundancy.

messages.author: George-GM1DSV
messages.text: Good morning all. Big developments this week. We're now meshing the Central Belt up into Angus. A wee bit patchy as it looks like the terrain is a tad bumpy. Sadly Perth is leapfrogged for now, bit I am sure @bosgag will have that sorted soon but that is another story. I've setup a temporary observer to help see what gets missed on the long stretch. Sure, we are a bit behind Englandshire (I am Geordie by birth before the starts flying), but their population density is far greater and closer together. One day, England will just be one big city whilst Scotland remains the garden of paradise .
messages.content_html: Good morning all. Big developments this week. We're now meshing the Central Belt up into Angus. A wee bit patchy as it looks like the terrain is a tad bumpy. Sadly Perth is leapfrogged for now, bit I am sure @bosgag will have that sorted soon but that is another story. I've setup a temporary observer to help see what gets missed on the long stretch. Sure, we are a bit behind Englandshire (I am Geordie by birth before the starts flying), but their population density is far greater and closer together. One day, England will just be one big city whilst Scotland remains the garden of paradise .

2
2

messages.author: SacrificeFly
messages.text: @bosgag @George-GM1DSV In Perth and just got my first node. Couldn't work out why I wasn't able to see the repeater but this seems to have explained it. Was delighted when I came here and seen the name @bosgag and talk of the repeater, I thought I was doing something wrong. I see there are two other repeaters on the map around Perth, do you know if these are active? Thinking about taking a day trip to Glasgow/Edinburgh just to test my node. Great to know areas north of Edinburgh are being working on!

messages.text: If you want a solar node, check out the sensecap Pro solar node at around 86 quid. They work well with the standard antenna and even better with an upgrade like a McGill

messages.author: George-GM1DSV
messages.text: Wee update: I have now got the DND (Dundee) region configured and I am the admin. The mesh map can be found at https://dnd.meshmapper.net where you will see how we are at the moment. We are also members of GLA (Glasgow) and EDI (Edinburgh) where GLA is Scotland wide. The region names come from the nearest airport to your location. The DND boundary is a tad rough just now but it is a very young mesh.

messages.author: Stevie_D (ROG)
messages.text: Created a Scotland channel on the new Discord. We can add regional channels in the future but we are less than 50 active users just now so let’s get all parts of Scotland connected first. A collective goal where we can identify isolated pockets of users and plan repeater deployment to link us all up.
